TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Greenskin Tribes Faction.

Translations.Comment: Pielverde combines 'piel' (skin) and 'verde' (green) to convey the essence of greenskins with a fantasy wargame tone.
Direct and forceful translation maintaining the brutal warrior aesthetic. 'Guerreros' is the standard term for warriors in Spanish fantasy contexts.
Brute force and savagery captured with 'Brutales'. Conveys the thuggish, aggressive nature appropriate for elite orc troops.
Jauría (horde/swarm) emphasizes the chaotic, disorganized nature of goblin forces. Trasgos is the Spanish term for goblins.
Lanceros (spear-wielders) clarifies the weapon specialty while maintaining the horde essence with Jauría.
Jabalí (wild boar) combined with Jinetes (riders) creates an appropriately savage and warlike unit name.
Combines Jinetes (riders) with Lobo (wolf) to convey a fast, predatory cavalry unit. Trasgos identifies the creature type.

Translations.Translation: Carro de Guerra Orco de Jabalí
Translations.Comment: Carro de Guerra (war chariot) emphasizes the military might and epic scale. The combination creates an imposing, destructive unit.
Arqueros (archers) combined with Arco Corto (shortbow) clearly identifies these as ranged troops. Direct and descriptive.
Virotes (bolts/crossbow bolts) combined with Lanzadores (throwers) captures the artillery aspect of this unit.
Lanzador de Piedras (stone thrower) is a straightforward, intimidating artillery designation consistent with classic fantasy wargaming.
Direct translation of the classic fantasy creature. Universally recognized in Spanish fantasy contexts.
Classic and powerful term for a colossal creature. Fits the mythical and epic scale of the fantasy wargame setting.
Guiverno is the traditional Spanish term for wyvern in fantasy literature and gaming, maintaining fantasy authenticity.
Combines Araña (spider) with Gigante to create an appropriately monstrous and fearsome creature designation.
Jefe de Guerra directly conveys tribal military leadership and authority. A powerful title befitting a legendary commander.
Chamán is the Spanish term for shaman, capturing the tribal mysticism and magical nature of goblin spellcasters.
Rey Trasgo (Goblin King) is the grand title for the faction leader, conveying both the goblin nature and royal authority.
